SEARCH
Package client.view

Class GateController

java.lang.Object
client.view.BaseController
client.view.GateController
public class GateController extends BaseController
The park-gate screen, visible to a PARK_EMPLOYEE only (gated in MainShellController; the server also re-checks the role on every op). An always-visible live-occupancy header sits above an Entry / Exit segmented toggle — only one mode's cards are shown at a time, so the screen fits without scrolling. Each mode pairs a reservation action with its casual counterpart:
  • Entry · reservation — confirmation code + visitor id → ENTER_VISIT.
  • Entry · casual walk-in — party size + visit type + optional visitor id → CASUAL_VISIT, showing the server-computed price and a ticket number (the visit id) for the employee to note.
  • Exit · reservation — confirmation code or visitor id → EXIT_VISIT.
  • Exit · casual walk-in — the ticket number issued at admission → EXIT_VISIT. This is the only way to exit an anonymous walk-in, which has no confirmation code or visitor id to look it up by.

The occupancy header refreshes on show, after every successful action, and on demand via the Refresh button. The employee's park is always derived server-side from the session, so no park id is sent from here. The casual price shown comes straight from the returned VisitDTO — it is never recomputed client-side.

Extends BaseController for navigation-lifecycle parity; it holds no push subscriptions (cross-client realtime occupancy push is a later session — refresh here is manual / after-action).

      Initializes the controller after its FXML fields are injected.

      Fetches live occupancy for the employee's park and repaints the header.

    • onEnter

      private void onEnter()

    • onExit

      private void onExit()

    • onCasualExit

      private void onCasualExit()
      Closes a casual walk-in by the ticket number issued at admission. This is the only way to exit an anonymous walk-in — it has no confirmation code or visitor id — so the visit id (shown as the ticket number on admit) is the handle.

    • onCasual

      private void onCasual()

    • showCasualTicket

      private void showCasualTicket(VisitDTO v)
      Renders the server-priced confirmation for a casual walk-in, leading with the ticket number (the visit id) for the employee to note — it is the only handle for exiting an anonymous walk-in later, under Exit · Casual. The total comes straight from the returned VisitDTO.getPriceCents() — never computed on the client.
      Parameters:
      v - the visit returned by the server

    • priceRow

      private javafx.scene.layout.HBox priceRow(String key, String value)
      Builds one key/value line for the casual price panel.

    • hideCasualPrice

      private void hideCasualPrice()
      Performs the hide casual price operation.

      private static String text(javafx.scene.control.TextField f)
